purpose: The commit adds a check to prevent queuing Bluetooth commands when the HCI_UNREGISTER flag is set, avoiding a use-after-free (UAF) error.
The commit removes an unnecessary include statement in n_hdlc.c to simplify the include hierarchy and improve code maintainability.
The commit updates documentation for a new 'N/A' store metric in perf-c2c, reflecting changes in report tables.
This commit fixes issues related to vma lock handling during splitting and unmapping in the hugetlb subsystem. It addresses race conditions and ensures proper lock management across VMAs.
The commit updates the devicetree bindings for the rt5682s codec by adding AVDD and MICVDD power supply definitions. These are required for proper hardware configuration.
Fixes a resource leak in zorro7xx_remove_one() by adding an iounmap() call to properly release resources.
The commit removes an unused 'work' field from a struct in the mlx5e_async_ctx structure, cleaning up unnecessary code.
The commit changes the type of key_idx from unsigned to signed in the cfg80211_auth_request struct to prevent static checker warnings when -1 is assigned.
The commit sorts the CONFIG_SURFACE_PLATFORMS in defconfig files to align with savedefconfig order, ensuring consistency without functional changes.
The commit introduces a helper function mcp251xfd_is_fd_mode() to replace open-coded checks for CAN-FD mode. It improves code maintainability by centralizing the condition check.

This commit modifies the RTC test suite to skip tests when the RTC device is not present. It adds checks in multiple test functions to handle cases where the RTC file descriptor is unavailable, preventing test failures in such scenarios.

Removes a redundant variable assignment that was causing a dead store warning in the compiler. The 'bytes' variable was assigned but never used, so this commit cleans up the code to eliminate the warning.
This commit modifies the rcuscale configuration to decouple it from RCU Tasks, allowing rcuscale to function without requiring RCU Tasks. This reduces unnecessary kernel size and complexity.
This commit fixes a duplicated phrase 'be split' in the spte.h file, correcting a typo in a comment.
Reverts a previous change that caused issues with clock table population, preventing underflow by reverting to an older method until a better solution is implemented.
The commit updates device tree bindings to include a compatible string for the Renesas RZ/V2L SoC with the Mali-G31 GPU.

This commit renames the struct pcie_port to dw_pcie_rp across multiple files to improve naming consistency within the DWC PCIe driver. The change affects function parameters, variable declarations, and structure definitions.
Fixes a clang warning by correcting a format specifier in debug logging to prevent incorrect type promotion handling.
Fixes a bug in the MIPS jump label handling where branch offset checks were incorrectly triggering warnings due to improper signed comparisons.

This commit removes a specific case from SetHwReg8188EU() and replaces direct function calls. It simplifies the code by eliminating redundant handling of HW_VAR_H2C_FW_P2P_PS_OFFLOAD, contributing to the removal of the hal layer.
The commit addresses sparse warnings by declaring non-global symbols as static in the arm64 kernel code. This change improves code quality by properly scoping variables.
The commit corrects an error in the device tree bindings by removing incorrect interrupt flags on MDSS nodes.
This commit modifies array allocation in struct ctrl_ref to prepare for dynamic size changes from drivers. It renames variables and adjusts array handling logic across multiple files.
The commit adds support for additional PMICs and SoCs to the socinfo driver, expanding its coverage of hardware platforms.

The commit improves the handling of chip versions in the Realtek wireless driver by recognizing all possible chip cuts (A-P) instead of showing 'unknown' for versions beyond E. It simplifies the code and enhances debugging information.
The commit removes redundant code related to the .is_enabled state in PRCMU clocks, as the core already manages the software enable count.
The commit aims to clean up and reorganize code by removing unnecessary #ifdef CONFIG_XIP_KERNEL usage, grouping similar cases together for better maintainability.
Enables QUP and GPI DMA hardware in the device tree to facilitate future I2C node additions.
The commit corrects comments and typos in the thermal driver code to improve clarity and adhere to coding style guidelines.
Fixes an infinite loop in release_swfw_sync by adding a conditional check to exit if the HW semaphore cannot be acquired.
The commit updates the pcm3168a driver to use modern ASoC DAI format terminology, replacing 'master_mode' with 'provider_mode' and adjusting related switch cases.
The commit removes a redundant code block that defined IS_SWAPFILE as 0 when not already defined. This change simplifies the codebase by eliminating unnecessary conditional checks.

This commit implements GTP echo response handling for both GTPv0 and GTPv1, ensuring compliance with GTP specifications. It adds necessary code to send Echo Response messages when Echo Requests are received, including setting up headers and information elements. Additionally, it introduces a new configuration option for the restart count.

The commit replaces the usage of cpumask_next_* functions with cpumask_first_* where appropriate. It introduces a new helper function cpumask_first_zero() and modifies several files to use these new functions instead of the previous ones.
The commit removes a call to 'dtim_assoc' IOVAR because the firmware no longer supports it. This prevents error messages when roaming between APs.
Adds support for a platform without an amplifier by introducing a new board configuration and machine setup.

The commit adds a check to fail zerocopy sendmsg operations when the socket does not support zero-copy functionality. It ensures that such requests are properly rejected by testing a specific bit flag in the socket's flags.
The commit modifies LaTeX documentation to check for the presence of xeCJK.sty before loading it, preventing 'xeCJK.sty not found!' errors on certain distributions.
Modifies checkpatch.pl to allow 'case' macros without reporting errors, preventing false positives for valid code.

This commit replaces the simple-KMS helpers with regular atomic helpers in the simpledrm driver. The changes involve restructuring how planes, CRTCs, and encoders are initialized and managed, improving code architecture for better maintainability.

This commit introduces a JPEG decode worker interface to enable parallel operation of three hardware units on the MT8195 platform. It adds functions for hardware management, including allocation, deallocation, parameter setting, and work distribution.
The commit removes unnecessary VPRINTK debug print statements from several ATA driver files, as their information is redundant with existing tracepoints.

This commit introduces support for entering low-power zstates during extended vblank periods in the AMD display driver. It modifies various components to enable this optimization, including adding new functions and updating existing ones to handle zstate transitions.
The commit fixes a type mismatch in the hqd_destroy() callback to resolve a Control Flow Integrity (CFI) failure when running on specific hardware with CONFIG_CFI_CLANG enabled.
The commit corrects the 'resets' property name in device tree bindings from 'reset' and adds schema validation.
The commit addresses an array-bounds warning in GCC 12 by adding compiler flags to suppress it unless W=1+ is set. It modifies the Makefile to include -Wno-array-bounds for mac.o.
The commit replaces pm_runtime_get_sync with pm_runtime_resume_and_get to simplify the code in sun8i-ss-prng.c.
This commit reorganizes fixed file handling in io_uring by splitting installation and removal into separate functions, improving code modularity and maintainability.
The commit updates device tree bindings to split G1 and G2 decoder nodes into separate entries, improving their representation in the hardware configuration.
The commit removes an unused macro and combines its contents into another macro to simplify the code structure.
